title: Energy-Efficient Server Virtualization
description: Algorithm to optimize server resource allocation for reduced energy consumption
code:
ALGORITHM EnergyEfficientVirtualization
BEGIN
 GET serverList[] // List of physical servers
 GET vmList[] // List of virtual machines
 
 FOR each server IN serverList
 currentLoad = GET CPUUsage(server)
 IF currentLoad < 30% THEN
 // Migrate VMs to fewer servers
 migratingVMs = SELECT VMsToMigrate(server, targetServer)
 FOR each vm IN migratingVMs
 MIGRATE(vm, targetServer)
 END FOR
 // Power down underutilized server
 POWER_OFF(server)
 ELSE IF currentLoad > 80% THEN
 // Add load balancing
 REBALANCE_LOAD(server)
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN energySaved
END
explanation: This algorithm reduces energy consumption by consolidating virtual machines onto fewer physical servers and powering off idle servers, demonstrating a key Green Computing strategy.

title: E-Waste Collection Route Optimization
description: Algorithm to optimize collection routes for e-waste pickup to minimize fuel consumption
code:
ALGORITHM OptimizeEwasteCollection
BEGIN
 GET pickupPoints[] // Locations requiring e-waste collection
 GET depot // Central collection depot
 SET vehicleCapacity = MAX_CAPACITY
 
 // Calculate optimal route using nearest neighbor heuristic
 currentLocation = depot
 route = []
 remainingCapacity = vehicleCapacity
 
 WHILE pickupPoints NOT EMPTY DO
 nearestPoint = FIND_NEAREST(currentLocation, pickupPoints)
 
 IF remainingCapacity >= nearestPoint.estimatedWeight THEN
 route.APPEND(nearestPoint)
 pickupPoints.REMOVE(nearestPoint)
 remainingCapacity = remainingCapacity - nearestPoint.estimatedWeight
 currentLocation = nearestPoint
 ELSE
 // Return to depot to unload
 route.APPEND(depot)
 remainingCapacity = vehicleCapacity
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 route.APPEND(depot) // Return to depot
 RETURN route
END
explanation: This algorithm optimizes e-waste collection routes to minimize travel distance and fuel consumption, contributing to sustainable waste management.
